dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4652 IS: Advancing Automation Research and Development in Agriculture Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Schiff introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry

form.official-title: To amend the Agricultural Research, Extension, and Education Reform Act of 1998 to reauthorize the specialty crop research initiative and establish a specialty crop mechanization and automation research and development program, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Advancing Automation Research and Development in Agriculture Act.

subsection.text: Section 412 of the Agricultural Research, Extension, and Education Reform Act of 1998 (7 U.S.C. 7632) is amended—

paragraph.text: in subsection (f)(3), by striking subsection (d) and (j), and inserting subsections (d), (j), and (k),;

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (3), by adding at the end the following:
The Secretary may waive the matching funds requirement under subparagraph (A).
in paragraph (4), by striking the Initiative and inserting this section;

paragraph.text: by redesignating subsections (j) and (k) as subsections (k) and (l), respectively;
by inserting after subsection (i) the following:

subsection.header: Specialty crop mechanization and automation research and development program

paragraph.text: The Secretary shall establish a competitive research and extension grant program to increase the competitiveness of specialty crops in the United States through the advancement and acceleration of mechanization and automation, under which the Secretary awards competitive grants to eligible entities—

subparagraph.text: to create or improve cost-effective mechanization, automation, and other innovations and technologies—

clause.text: to reduce the manual labor requirements of a specialty crop grower;
to improve specialty crop farmworker safety and health or working conditions; and
to increase the efficiency of specialty crop—

subparagraph.text: to increase the adoption of specialty crop mechanization, automation, and other innovations and technologies by—

clause.text: emphasizing adoption drivers, which may include connectivity, autonomy, reliability, durability, in-field validation, and cost-effectiveness; or
investing in and developing human capital to increase the capacity of the specialty crop sector to work with new technologies; or

subparagraph.text: to accelerate specialty crop mechanization and automation through—

paragraph.text: In awarding grants under this subsection, the Secretary shall give priority to proposals for projects that—

subparagraph.text: address the training or retraining of farmworkers (including to maintain adequate worker safety) to operate, repair, program, or otherwise maintain mechanization and automation solutions involved in the project; and
include explicit mechanisms to communicate the results of the project to producers and the public.

paragraph.text: Subsections (a), (c) through (g), (i), and (l) shall apply to the program established under this subsection.
in subsection (k) (as so redesignated), in paragraph (5), by striking In addition to the amounts reserved under subsection (k)(1)(C), there and inserting There; and

subparagraph.text: in paragraph (1), by striking subparagraphs (C) and (D) and inserting the following:

For each of fiscal years 2026 through 2031, the Secretary shall reserve not less than $30,000,000 of the funds made available under subparagraph (B) to carry out the program established under subsection (j).

Notwithstanding paragraph (4), any funds reserved under subparagraph (C) that remain unobligated at the end of the fiscal year following the fiscal year in which such funds are first made available shall be reallocated to carry out the Initiative.
in paragraph (2)—

clause.text: in the paragraph heading, by striking for fiscal years 2014 through 2023;
by striking In addition and inserting the following:

subparagraph.text: In addition

clause.text: in subparagraph (A) (as so designated), by striking 2023 and inserting 2031;

subparagraph.text: by redesignating paragraph (3) as subparagraph (B) of paragraph (2) and indenting appropriately; and
by redesignating paragraphs (4) and (5) as paragraphs (3) and (4), respectively.

paragraph.text: Section 1408A of the National Agricultural Research, Extension, and Teaching Policy Act of 1977 (7 U.S.C. 3123a) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in subsection (a)(2)(A), by striking (j)(3) and inserting (k)(3);
in subsection (c)(5), by striking specialty crop research initiative and inserting programs; and
in subsection (g), in the matter preceding paragraph (1), by striking (j) and inserting (k).

paragraph.text: Section 251(f)(1)(D)(x) of the Department of Agriculture Reorganization Act of 1994 (7 U.S.C. 6971(f)(1)(D)(x)) is amended by striking specialty crop research initiative under section 412 of the Agricultural Research, Extension, and Education Reform Act of 1998 and inserting programs under section 412 of the Agricultural Research, Extension, and Education Reform Act of 1998 (7 U.S.C. 7632).
Section 12605(c) of the Agriculture Improvement Act of 2018 (7 U.S.C. 7632 note; Public Law 115–334) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by striking 412(j) and inserting 412(k); and
by striking 7632(j)) and inserting 7632(k)).
